extra.reasoning: The only transition that sets sent:=true is t_send_allowed, whose guard is "not opted_out". The only transition that sets opted_out is t_optout, which sets opted_out:=true and is monotone — no event ever clears opted_out (there is no opt-back-in). Therefore once opted_out is true it stays true, t_send_allowed is permanently disabled, and every send_attempt instead matches t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) which sets sent:=false. t_optout itself also sets sent:=false at the moment of opt-out. All key_0/key_1/key_2 stage transitions (the combination-lock advance/reset rules over stage 0..8) only ever set sent:=false and never touch opted_out; the send transitions never depend on stage, so the lock is a decoy with no bearing on the property. Hence in every reachable state, opted_out true implies sent false. The invariant globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all executions (well within the 11-event bound).
